constellations.name: Ursa Minor
constellations.blurb: Polaris anchors the little dipper. It sits almost exactly above the north pole — your anchor for finding everything else.

constellations.name: Ursa Major
constellations.blurb: The Big Dipper's 'pointer stars' Dubhe and Merak line up to Polaris. Draw the line outward from the Dipper's bowl to find north.

constellations.name: Orion
constellations.blurb: Orion's belt — Mintaka, Alnilam, Alnitak — runs east–west. Down from the belt hangs the sword with the Orion Nebula.

constellations.name: Lyra
constellations.blurb: Vega is the fifth-brightest star in the night sky and the northern anchor of the Summer Triangle.

constellations.name: Aquila
constellations.blurb: Altair is the southern apex of the Summer Triangle, spinning rapidly just 17 light-years away.

constellations.name: Cygnus
constellations.blurb: Deneb marks the tail of the swan and the third corner of the Summer Triangle.

constellations.name: Canis Major
constellations.blurb: Sirius, the Dog Star, is the brightest star visible from Earth — a winter beacon south of Orion.

constellations.name: Taurus
constellations.blurb: Aldebaran is the red eye of the bull, glowing through the V-shaped Hyades cluster.

constellations.name: Auriga
constellations.blurb: Capella is a yellow giant, the sixth-brightest star in the sky, high overhead on winter nights.

constellations.name: Boötes
constellations.blurb: Follow the Big Dipper's handle in an arc to Arcturus, an orange giant preparing to bloom.

constellations.name: Scorpius
constellations.blurb: Antares — the rival of Mars — is a red supergiant at the scorpion's heart.

constellations.name: Virgo
constellations.blurb: Continue Arcturus' arc to Spica, a hot blue-white binary low in the south on spring nights.

constellations.name: Canis Minor
constellations.blurb: Procyon rises before Sirius and forms the third vertex of the Winter Triangle with Betelgeuse.

constellations.name: Gemini
constellations.blurb: Castor and Pollux are the twin heads, side by side, east of Auriga.

constellations.name: Leo
constellations.blurb: Regulus anchors the lion's heart; Denebola marks its tail. The sickle of Leo curves above Regulus.
